messages.author: UP
messages.text: I know there have been a few nodes in the Atlanta area, but let's get some infrastructure going. Freq, bw, and sf need to match in order to participate in the same network, but cr is interoperable. Most of our stuff is on the narrow settings (USA recommended as of meshcore 1.8), though I recomend to tweak the Coding Rate to 8 instead of 5 until we start getting some density and connections. Frequency 910.525 Bandwidth 62.5 Spreading Factor 7 Coding Rate 8 All companion or messaging nodes , please add a hashtag channel named #test If you are deploying or updating a router , please set regions "ATL" and "us-ga" with region flooding allowed for each region. If near a state line, also add the other applicable states with naming format us-pc, where pc is the two letter postal code of the state. Further instructions on regionalizing will probably follow soon. (edited)
messages.content_html: I know there have been a few nodes in the Atlanta area, but let's get some infrastructure going. Freq, bw, and sf need to match in order to participate in the same network, but cr is interoperable. Most of our stuff is on the narrow settings (USA recommended as of meshcore 1.8), though I recomend to tweak the Coding Rate to 8 instead of 5 until we start getting some density and connections. Frequency 910.525 Bandwidth 62.5 Spreading Factor 7 Coding Rate 8 All companion or messaging nodes, please add a hashtag channel named #test If you are deploying or updating a router, please set regions "ATL" and "us-ga" with region flooding allowed for each region. If near a state line, also add the other applicable states with naming format us-pc, where pc is the two letter postal code of the state. Further instructions on regionalizing will probably follow soon. (edited)
messages.reactions_html: 2

messages.author: UP
messages.text: I climbed Stone Mountain yesterday in hopes of deploying a repeater, but could not ping anything from up top, despite that we have several nodes around town now. I'm going to try Kennesaw mt today, and see if that has a better angle for connecting the nodes that exist already. I hope to build some decent repeaters and the deploy them over the fall and winter seasons, but the only hardware I have available right at this moment is not great, hence the name "Droopy." For anyone local who may read this in the coming month: please go easy on poor li'l Droopy, and know that beefier infrastructure is on the way. (edited)
messages.content_html: I climbed Stone Mountain yesterday in hopes of deploying a repeater, but could not ping anything from up top, despite that we have several nodes around town now. I'm going to try Kennesaw mt today, and see if that has a better angle for connecting the nodes that exist already. I hope to build some decent repeaters and the deploy them over the fall and winter seasons, but the only hardware I have available right at this moment is not great, hence the name "Droopy." For anyone local who may read this in the coming month: please go easy on poor li'l Droopy, and know that beefier infrastructure is on the way. (edited)

messages.author: UP
messages.text: Update: Droopy's battery seems to have given out, and I'm rather concerned about the 500kHz bandwidth requirement we've blissfully overlooked. In the meanwhile, however, I did have an idea that could be helpful: I will be setting Coding Rate to 8 on any LoRa deployments going forward. Unlike frequency, Bandwidth, and Spreading Factor, which fundamentally break compatibility if any of them don't match, Coding rate is the level of error correction used to transmit, and devices with different coding rates can talk to each other. My rationale is that making a breaking change in an existing network is always difficult, and as meshes form and grow, the concern starts out being whether we can reach each other, and later becomes congestion. Right now, with very few meshcore neighbors, we need to reach farther, but later, it will become difficult to change breaking parameters, so I think that right now, regardless of what other parameters are determined to address the regulatory issues, we should max out the nonbreaking one until density arrives. (edited)
messages.content_html: Update: Droopy's battery seems to have given out, and I'm rather concerned about the 500kHz bandwidth requirement we've blissfully overlooked. In the meanwhile, however, I did have an idea that could be helpful: I will be setting Coding Rate to 8 on any LoRa deployments going forward. Unlike frequency, Bandwidth, and Spreading Factor, which fundamentally break compatibility if any of them don't match, Coding rate is the level of error correction used to transmit, and devices with different coding rates can talk to each other. My rationale is that making a breaking change in an existing network is always difficult, and as meshes form and grow, the concern starts out being whether we can reach each other, and later becomes congestion. Right now, with very few meshcore neighbors, we need to reach farther, but later, it will become difficult to change breaking parameters, so I think that right now, regardless of what other parameters are determined to address the regulatory issues, we should max out the nonbreaking one until density arrives. (edited)

messages.author: IonicFrog
messages.text: I dont think the meshcore would work well with moving repeaters. My understanding is that it would mess up paths. If the path to between two contacts is a-b-c and your car is b. when the next message is sent and b is not reachable because it moved then a new path would have to be found

messages.text: Glad to have you! And yeah, terrain over there is a plate. Not a lot of vantage points to get LOS, so the whole south metro has usually been a dead zone for mesh stuff. With some well placed MeshCore routers, though, it should be possible to establish pockets of conectivity soon.

messages.author: UP
messages.text: I think I had seen that written somewhere, but never encountered it in practice while experimenting with router configuration. The console can be used within the android app to add regions to a repeater without that restriction, even remotely over an outdated companion. If you watch Andy's region demo video, he's using capitalized regions, so this sounds like a bug, but I need to update my companion firmware in order to even have a chance of reproducing it, so I'll try that soon.
messages.content_html: I think I had seen that written somewhere, but never encountered it in practice while experimenting with router configuration. The console can be used within the android app to add regions to a repeater without that restriction, even remotely over an outdated companion. If you watch Andy's region demo video, he's using capitalized regions, so this sounds like a bug, but I need to update my companion firmware in order to even have a chance of reproducing it, so I'll try that soon.

messages.author: mledford
messages.text: Brand new user here and I have what is probably a silly question. I'm in southern Gwinnett about 2.5 miles from two repeaters. Looking at letsmesh.net I see that one repeater gets picked up at another repeater much much further away. I thought for sure I might be able to pick up this repeater but only if I travel closer to it was I able to pick it up. Granted I have a Heltec v4.3 board with a simple dipole antenna indoors on a second floor. I've now tried two different antennas though to be fair they are all from Amazon so probably not the best quality. I was hoping to proof of concept being able to at least receive before attempting to get better equipment to possibly deploy a repeater. Unfortunately I don't have a good way to get a node much higher. Just trying to figure out how to proceed. If anyone has any thoughts or suggestions let me know. Thanks!

messages.author: mledford
messages.text: Yes. I tried this one first. https://a.co/d/00HTmWD5 I tried this one today. (Vertically I don’t have it bent) https://a.co/d/01bS84rL I also received this one which looks like the first one. However, I was hoping the previous one would work so I haven’t broken the seal on that bag yet. https://a.co/d/09jn5AtF The node is near a window but not directly at it. I did take the node outside at one point for a bit on an upstairs porch but didn’t seem to help. I know the new Heltec boards had some problems and I’ve run the just released firmware along with the custom Heltec firmware. Dunno if there is some different settings I should try but I don’t see any in the official meshOS app. Or if I should use different hardware.

embeds_html:
Meshtastic Antenna
Product Description for 915MHz Antenna 10dBi Gain Elevate your connectivity with our 915MHz Antenna 10dBi Gain, specifically designed for Meshtastic LoRa applications. This high-performance omni-directional whip antenna boasts a remarkable 10dBi gain, ensuring superior range and reliability for y...

RF Antenna
2 Pack 915MHz LORA Antenna 5dBi Gain Omni SMA Male Connector + RF Antenna Adapter Cable

Muzi 915Mhz Whip Antenna - Upgrade Your LoRa and Meshtastic Perform...
Upgrade your antenna to this flexible whip design. Improves performance, measured SWR of 1.3 compared with the stock stubby antenna at a SWR of 3.5. SWR is a measure of transmission efficiency, how much power from the LoRa radio makes it out into the world via the antenna. SWR 1.3 = 98% efficient...

messages.author: mledford
messages.text: I did get two transmissions last night. However the noise floor shown on the device is really high now. In the -50 to -60 range. It’s been sitting at -85 to -95. Once I saw that I suspect that my radio is damaged. Checking the U.FL to SMA connector I discovered that while the center is connected through the ground is not. I don’t know if this could damage the radio like having no antenna connected or not. I have hopefully better quality connectors and a new board on order. We’ll see where that leads.
messages.content_html: I did get two transmissions last night. However the noise floor shown on the device is really high now. In the -50 to -60 range. It’s been sitting at -85 to -95. Once I saw that I suspect that my radio is damaged. Checking the U.FL to SMA connector I discovered that while the center is connected through the ground is not. I don’t know if this could damage the radio like having no antenna connected or not. I have hopefully better quality connectors and a new board on order. We’ll see where that leads.

messages.author: mledford
messages.text: I haven’t fixed the connector I have one on order. It was -95 but this morning it’s -50. Putting on the little pigtail antenna that Heltec ships with the device still has it that high. Now it bounces around but still very high.
messages.content_html: I haven’t fixed the connector I have one on order. It was -95 but this morning it’s -50. Putting on the little pigtail antenna that Heltec ships with the device still has it that high. Now it bounces around but still very high.

messages.author: mledford
messages.text: I found why it was so high. I flashed this morning and forgot to reset the correct radio settings.
messages.content_html: I found why it was so high. I flashed this morning and forgot to reset the correct radio settings.

messages.author: mledford
messages.text: I have a better noise floor than before. Now it sits at -90 to -95. Though I see it go up to -75 at times. This is with the new antenna connector. I have a different antenna and board coming tomorrow. Will continue to try. Thanks! (edited)
messages.content_html: I have a better noise floor than before. Now it sits at -90 to -95. Though I see it go up to -75 at times. This is with the new antenna connector. I have a different antenna and board coming tomorrow. Will continue to try. Thanks! (edited)
